title:: Florence Cathedral Dome
tags:: brunelleschi, dome, florence, quattrocento

# Florence Cathedral Dome

The dome of the Cattedrale di Santa Maria del Fiore in Florence, designed
by [[Filippo Brunelleschi]] and completed in 1436, spans 45.5 metres at
its inner diameter. Its [[Double-Shell Construction]] technique
eliminated the need for a centring framework and made the project
buildable within the resources of the Quattrocento guilds.

The structure was made possible by Brunelleschi's reading of Roman
precedents, most notably the [[Pantheon Rome]], although the geometry
he chose — a pointed quinto acuto profile — is structurally distinct.
